data.body:
These are the garments worn by kings and queens during the Joseon Dynasty in Korea.
They embody the Korean spirit.
They differ from those of China and Japan, which are also part of East Asia.
They are on display at the National Palace Museum of Korea in Seoul.

data.body: uh, well... she'd been a stray kitten that found her way here over the winter. to prepare for summer, we decided to fill in the ground hog holes around the house. then, he didn't show up with the old porch cat for food... one day was fine, two day was odd...
data.author: did:plc:ru4ykzorzkyv64ajhk73wcu4

data.body: on day three, we were sitting by the open window and heard a faint meowing. with flashlights, we went to investigate and discovered just her nose sticking out of the dirt. she must have used the groundhog hole to hide out in the house's old stone foundation and dug herself out when it was filled it.
data.author: did:plc:ru4ykzorzkyv64ajhk73wcu4

data.body: we got the shovel and dug her the rest of the way out... I haven't ever seen a cat look so shell-shocked before, poor girl.
data.author: did:plc:ru4ykzorzkyv64ajhk73wcu4
